words.definitionEn: happening or done without delay
words.exampleEn: an immediate reaction/response

words.definitionEn: a person who has come to live permanently in a different country from the one they were born in
words.exampleEn: immigrant from…, immigrants from other European countries

words.definitionEn: the powerful effect that something has on somebody/something
words.exampleEn: a positive/a negative/an adverse impact

words.definitionEn: to have an effect on somebody/something
words.exampleEn: impact on/upon somebody/something, Her father's death impacted greatly on her childhood years.

words.definitionEn: to say that you are sorry for doing something wrong or causing a problem
words.exampleEn: Why should I apologize?

words.definitionEn: a product or service that is brought into one country from another
words.exampleEn: import from…, food imports from abroad

words.definitionEn: to bring a product, a service, an idea, etc. into one country from another
words.exampleEn: import something, The country has to import most of its raw materials.

words.definitionEn: the quality of being important
words.exampleEn: She stressed the importance of careful preparation.

words.definitionEn: an idea, a feeling or an opinion that you get about somebody/something, or that somebody/something gives you
words.exampleEn: My first impression of him was favourable.

words.definitionEn: making you admire them, because they are very large, good, skilful, etc.
words.exampleEn: an impressive performance

words.definitionEn: the act of making something better; the process of something becoming better
words.exampleEn: The economy has shown significant improvement over the past 9 months.

words.definitionEn: extremely
words.exampleEn: It was all incredibly difficult.

words.definitionEn: used after very and an adjective or adverb to emphasize a statement, description, etc.
words.exampleEn: Thank you very much indeed!

words.definitionEn: to show that something is true or exists
words.exampleEn: indicate something, Initial tests indicate the presence of oxygen.

words.definitionEn: happening not as the main aim, cause or result of a particular action, but in addition to it
words.exampleEn: the indirect effects of the war

words.definitionEn: located, done or used inside a building
words.exampleEn: an indoor swimming pool

words.definitionEn: inside or into a building
words.exampleEn: I prefer to stay indoors in this really hot weather.

words.definitionEn: the effect that somebody/something has on the way a person thinks or behaves or on the way that something works or develops
words.exampleEn: His early work shows the influence of Cézanne and Matisse.

words.definitionEn: to have an effect on the way that somebody behaves or thinks, especially by giving them an example to follow
words.exampleEn: influence somebody/something, His writings have influenced the lives of millions.

words.definitionEn: one of the things from which something is made, especially one of the foods that are used together to make a particular dish
words.exampleEn: Mix all the ingredients in a bowl.

words.definitionEn: to harm yourself or somebody else physically, especially in an accident
words.exampleEn: He injured his knee playing hockey.

words.definitionEn: physically hurt; having an injury
words.exampleEn: His injured leg prevented him from walking.

words.definitionEn: not guilty of a crime, etc.; not having done something wrong
words.exampleEn: They have imprisoned an innocent man.

words.definitionEn: the ability to learn, understand and think in a logical way about things; the ability to do this well
words.exampleEn: a person of high/average/low intelligence

words.definitionEn: to have a plan, result or purpose in your mind when you do something
words.exampleEn: We finished later than we had intended.

words.definitionEn: what you intend or plan to do; your aim
words.exampleEn: intention of doing something, I have no intention of going to the wedding.

words.definitionEn: a formal (often written) request for something, such as a job, permission to do something or a place at a college or university
words.exampleEn: a planning/patent/visa application

words.definitionEn: to buy property, shares in a company, etc. in the hope of making a profit
words.exampleEn: Now is a good time to invest.

words.definitionEn: to carefully examine the facts of a situation, an event, a crime, etc. to find out the truth about it or how it happened
words.exampleEn: The FBI has been called in to investigate.

words.definitionEn: a formal arrangement to meet or visit somebody at a particular time, especially for a reason connected with their work
words.exampleEn: I've got a dental appointment at 3 o'clock.

words.definitionEn: taking part in something; being part of something or connected with something
words.exampleEn: Some people tried to stop the fight but I didn't want to get involved.

words.definitionEn: to recognize the good qualities of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Over the years he came to appreciate the beauty and tranquillity of the river.

words.definitionEn: a chemical element. Iron is a hard strong metal that is used to make steel and is also found in small quantities in blood and food.
words.exampleEn: iron gates/bars/railings

words.definitionEn: to make clothes, etc. smooth by using an iron
words.exampleEn: He was ironing when I arrived.

words.definitionEn: an important topic that people are discussing or arguing about
words.exampleEn: a key/major issue

words.definitionEn: the study and use of electronic processes and equipment to store and send information of all kinds, including words, pictures and numbers (the abbreviation for ‘information technology’)
words.exampleEn: the increasing use of IT in all aspects of today's society

words.definitionEn: a newspaper or magazine that deals with a particular subject or profession
words.exampleEn: a scientific/an academic journal

words.definitionEn: a person in a court who has the authority to decide how criminals should be punished or to make legal decisions
words.exampleEn: a High Court judge

words.definitionEn: to form an opinion about somebody/something, based on the information you have
words.exampleEn: As far as I can judge, all of them are to blame.

words.definitionEn: wanting to do something or wanting something to happen very much
words.exampleEn: keen to do something, John was very keen to help.

words.definitionEn: the opportunity or right to use something or to see somebody/something
words.exampleEn: High-speed internet access has become a necessity.

words.definitionEn: to put information into a computer using a keyboard
words.exampleEn: key something, I was busy keying data.

words.definitionEn: the set of keys for operating a computer or typewriter, or the set of letters that you can touch to write on a smartphone or tablet
words.exampleEn: The program locks the keyboard until a password is given.

words.definitionEn: a movement with the foot or the leg, usually to hit something with the foot
words.exampleEn: the first kick of the game

words.definitionEn: to hit somebody/something with your foot
words.exampleEn: Stop kicking—it hurts!

words.definitionEn: an act of killing somebody deliberately
words.exampleEn: Their leader condemned the torture and killing of innocent civilians.

words.definitionEn: caring about others; gentle, friendly and generous
words.exampleEn: a very kind and helpful person

words.definitionEn: the act of kissing somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Come here and give me a kiss!

words.definitionEn: to touch somebody with your lips as a sign of love or sexual desire or when saying hello or goodbye
words.exampleEn: They stood in a doorway kissing (= kissing each other).

words.definitionEn: used to show that something is almost, but not completely, accurate or correct
words.exampleEn: The journey took approximately seven hours.

words.definitionEn: the sound of somebody hitting a door, window, etc. with their hand or with something hard to attract attention
words.exampleEn: knock on/at something, There was a knock at the door.

words.definitionEn: a piece of paper, etc. that is attached to something and that gives information about it
words.exampleEn: Always read the label carefully.

words.definitionEn: to fix a label on something or write information on something
words.exampleEn: label something, Make sure that your luggage is clearly labelled.

words.definitionEn: a room or building used for scientific research, experiments, testing, etc.
words.exampleEn: a clinical/research laboratory

words.definitionEn: the state of not having something or not having enough of something
words.exampleEn: lack of something, a lack of understanding/knowledge

words.definitionEn: to have none or not enough of something
words.exampleEn: to lack confidence/experience/resources/power

words.definitionEn: the most recent or newest
words.exampleEn: the latest craze/fashion/trend

words.definitionEn: to put somebody/something in a particular position, especially when it is done gently or carefully
words.exampleEn: lay somebody/something + adv./prep., He laid a hand on my arm.

words.definitionEn: a quantity or sheet of something that lies over a surface or between surfaces
words.exampleEn: layer (of something), A thin layer of dust covered everything.

words.definitionEn: most important or most successful
words.exampleEn: a leading expert/authority/figure/member

words.definitionEn: a flat green part of a plant, growing from a stem or branch or from the root
words.exampleEn: lettuce/cabbage/oak leaves

words.definitionEn: material made by removing the hair or fur from animal skins and preserving the skins using special processes
words.exampleEn: a leather jacket

words.definitionEn: to open a computer file or use a computer system
words.exampleEn: Most people use their phones to access the internet.

words.definitionEn: connected with the law
words.exampleEn: the legal profession/system

words.definitionEn: time when you are not working or studying; free time
words.exampleEn: These days we have more money and more leisure to enjoy it.

words.definitionEn: the size or measurement of something from one end to the other
words.exampleEn: Measure the length of the line from A to B.

words.definitionEn: having a flat surface that does not slope
words.exampleEn: Pitch the tent on level ground.

words.definitionEn: a statement made by somebody knowing that it is not true
words.exampleEn: to tell a lie

words.definitionEn: to say or write something that you know is not true
words.exampleEn: You could see from his face that he was lying.

words.definitionEn: the things that you like
words.exampleEn: We all have different likes and dislikes.

words.definitionEn: the greatest or smallest amount of something that is allowed
words.exampleEn: They imposed a strict spending limit.

words.definitionEn: to stop something from increasing beyond a particular amount or level
words.exampleEn: measures to limit carbon dioxide emissions

words.definitionEn: either of the two soft edges at the opening to the mouth
words.exampleEn: The assistant pursed her lips.

words.definitionEn: in the form of a liquid; not a solid or a gas
words.exampleEn: liquid nitrogen

words.definitionEn: a substance that flows freely and is not a solid or a gas, for example water or oil
words.exampleEn: She poured the dark brown liquid down the sink.

words.definitionEn: pieces of writing that are valued as works of art, especially novels, plays and poems (in contrast to technical books and newspapers, magazines, etc.)
words.exampleEn: English/American/French literature

words.definitionEn: living; not dead
words.exampleEn: live animals

words.definitionEn: broadcast at the time of an actual event; played or recorded at an actual performance
words.exampleEn: The show is going out live.

words.definitionEn: alive now
words.exampleEn: all living things

words.definitionEn: money to buy the things that you need in life
words.exampleEn: She earns her living as a freelance journalist.

words.definitionEn: a person who lives in a particular place or district
words.exampleEn: The locals are very friendly.

words.definitionEn: to find the exact position of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: The mechanic located the fault immediately.

words.definitionEn: if something is located in a particular place, it exists there or has been put there
words.exampleEn: a small town located 30 miles south of Chicago

words.definitionEn: a place where something happens or exists; the position of something
words.exampleEn: a honeymoon in a secret location

words.definitionEn: unhappy because you have no friends or people to talk to
words.exampleEn: She lives alone and often feels lonely.

words.definitionEn: the state of no longer having something or as much of something; the process that leads to this
words.exampleEn: I want to report the loss of a package.

words.definitionEn: expensive and of very high quality
words.exampleEn: a luxury hotel/car/apartment

words.definitionEn: the fact of enjoying special and expensive things, particularly food and drink, clothes and places
words.exampleEn: a life of unimaginable luxury

words.definitionEn: the act of arresting somebody
words.exampleEn: The police made several arrests.

words.definitionEn: very stupid; not at all sensible
words.exampleEn: You must be mad to risk it.

words.definitionEn: having or using special powers to make impossible things happen or seem to happen
words.exampleEn: a magic spell/charm/potion

words.definitionEn: the secret power of appearing to make impossible things happen by saying special words or doing special things
words.exampleEn: Do you believe in magic?

words.definitionEn: if the police arrest somebody, the person is taken to a police station and kept there because the police believe they may be guilty of a crime
words.exampleEn: arrest somebody, A man has been arrested in connection with the robbery.

words.definitionEn: more than anything else; also used to talk about the most important reason for something
words.exampleEn: They eat mainly fruit and nuts.

words.definitionEn: an act of coming or being brought to a place
words.exampleEn: We apologize for the late arrival of the train.

words.definitionEn: a large building or covered area that has many shops, restaurants, etc. inside it
words.exampleEn: Let's go to the mall.

words.definitionEn: the activity of running and controlling a business or similar organization
words.exampleEn: a career in management

words.definitionEn: to advertise a product in a particular way in order to make people want it
words.exampleEn: market something, The company utilizes every media tool available to market its products.

words.definitionEn: the activity of presenting, advertising and selling a company’s products or services in the best possible way
words.exampleEn: a marketing campaign/strategy

words.definitionEn: the legal relationship between two people who are married to each other
words.exampleEn: a happy/an unhappy marriage

words.definitionEn: while something else is happening
words.exampleEn: Leave the cake to cool completely. Meanwhile, make the topping.

words.definitionEn: an official action that is done in order to achieve a particular aim
words.exampleEn: safety/austerity measures

words.definitionEn: to find the size, quantity, etc. of something in standard units
words.exampleEn: measure something/somebody, a device that measures the level of radiation in the atmosphere

words.definitionEn: in the middle between a larger and smaller size, amount, length, temperature, etc.
words.exampleEn: There are three sizes—small, medium and large.

words.definitionEn: connected with or happening in the mind; involving the process of thinking
words.exampleEn: the mental process of remembering

words.definitionEn: an act of referring to somebody/something in speech or writing
words.exampleEn: He made no mention of her work.

words.definitionEn: a dirty or untidy state
words.exampleEn: in a mess, The room was in a mess.

words.definitionEn: not severe or strong
words.exampleEn: a mild form of the disease

words.definitionEn: a deep hole or holes under the ground where minerals such as coal, gold, etc. are dug
words.exampleEn: a copper/diamond mine

words.definitionEn: a combination of different people or things
words.exampleEn: It's a school with a good social and ethnic mix of children.

words.definitionEn: if two or more substances or things mix or you mix them, they combine, usually in a way that means they cannot easily be separated
words.exampleEn: Oil and water do not mix.

words.definitionEn: a combination of different things
words.exampleEn: She was a curious mixture, part grand lady, part wild child.

words.definitionEn: the way you are feeling at a particular time
words.exampleEn: in a … mood, She's in a good mood today (= happy and friendly).

words.definitionEn: a change of place or position
words.exampleEn: Don't make a move!

words.definitionEn: wet earth that is soft and sticky
words.exampleEn: The car wheels got stuck in the mud.

words.definitionEn: the crime of killing somebody deliberately
words.exampleEn: He was found guilty of murder.

words.definitionEn: to kill somebody deliberately and illegally
words.exampleEn: He denies murdering his wife's lover.

words.definitionEn: a piece of body tissue that you make tight and relax in order to move a particular part of the body; the tissue that forms the muscles of the body
words.exampleEn: a calf/neck/thigh muscle

words.definitionEn: a play or film in which part or all of the story is told using songs and often dancing
words.exampleEn: a Broadway/Hollywood musical

words.definitionEn: something that is difficult to understand or to explain
words.exampleEn: It is one of the great unsolved mysteries of this century.

words.definitionEn: the thin hard layer covering the outer tip of the fingers or toes
words.exampleEn: Stop biting your nails!

words.definitionEn: describing events or telling a story
words.exampleEn: narrative fiction

words.definitionEn: a description of events
words.exampleEn: a gripping narrative of their journey up the Amazon

words.definitionEn: a country considered as a group of people with the same language, culture and history, who live in a particular area under one government
words.exampleEn: European/Arab/Asian nations

words.definitionEn: connected with the place where you were born and lived for the first years of your life
words.exampleEn: your native land/country/city

words.definitionEn: a person who was born in a particular country or area
words.exampleEn: a native of New York

words.definitionEn: in a way that you would expect
words.exampleEn: Naturally, I get upset when things go wrong.

words.definitionEn: used to say that something cannot be avoided
words.exampleEn: The number of places available is necessarily limited.

words.definitionEn: used to state that something is/was not necessary or that only very little is/was necessary; used to ask if something is/was necessary
words.exampleEn: need (not) do something, You needn't bother asking Rick—I know he's too busy.

words.definitionEn: a small thin piece of steel that you use for sewing, with a point at one end and a hole for the thread at the other
words.exampleEn: a needle and thread

words.definitionEn: a district or an area of a town; the people who live there
words.exampleEn: We grew up in the same neighbourhood.

words.definitionEn: used to show that a negative statement is also true of somebody/something else
words.exampleEn: He didn't remember and neither did I.

words.definitionEn: material that is made of string, thread or wire twisted or tied together, with small spaces in between; a piece of this material used for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: Unfortunately the animals are often caught in fishing nets.

words.definitionEn: a task or piece of work that somebody is given to do, usually as part of their job or studies
words.exampleEn: Students are required to complete all homework assignments.
